learningObjective: Understand what it means for a function to approach a value as x gets closer to a point
ahaMoment: As you zoom in infinitely, the curve flattens toward a horizontal line at the limit value

miniSpec.title: Infinite Zoom / Magnifying Glass
miniSpec.primary_visual_feedback: ViewBox shrinks around target point, curve appears to flatten toward limit value
miniSpec.causal_chain_exposed: Higher zoom → smaller window → curve looks more linear → approaches horizontal line at y=4
miniSpec.aha_moment: No matter how far you zoom, the function stays near y=4 at x=2

modelVariables.limitZoomLevel: zoom level controlling how zoomed in the view is (1-100)
modelVariables.limitCurrentFx: derived: the current f(x) value displayed

assertions.description: On load, the zoom is at 1x showing the full curve, not yet zoomed to see the limit clearly
assertions.visualCheck: Full parabola visible, limit not yet obvious from curve shape

assertions.description: Increasing zoom level shrinks the visible window around the target point
assertions.visualCheck: Window shrinks to ±0.4 units around (2,4), curve appears flatter

assertions.description: At high zoom, the curve appears nearly horizontal at the limit value
assertions.visualCheck: Curve appears almost horizontal, green dashed line at y=4 is dominant
